title: Implement TxData provenance types for C5 traceability
description:
# TxData and Provenance Types for C5 Traceability (INV-STORE-014)

## Task
Implement the transaction metadata type that ensures every command produces traceable provenance.

## TxData (guide/types.md canonical)
```rust
pub struct TxData {
    pub tx_entity: EntityId,           // entity ID of this transaction
    pub provenance: ProvenanceType,    // how this knowledge was obtained
    pub causal_predecessors: Vec<TxId>, // what transactions this depends on
    pub agent: AgentId,                // who created this
    pub rationale: String,             // human-readable why
}
```

## ProvenanceType Lattice (spec/01-store.md §1.7)
```rust
pub enum ProvenanceType {
    Hypothesized,  // least certain: agent guess
    Inferred,      // derived from existing data
    Derived,       // computed from rules
    Observed,      // most certain: directly witnessed
}
```
Lattice order: Hypothesized < Inferred < Derived < Observed.
This forms a total order (chain), making it a trivial lattice.

## TxReceipt (returned by Store::transact)
```rust
pub struct TxReceipt {
    pub tx_id: TxId,
    pub datom_count: usize,
    pub new_entities: Vec<EntityId>,
    pub provenance: ProvenanceType,
}
```

## C5 Traceability Requirement
EVERY transaction must carry provenance. No transaction may be created without:
- A valid AgentId
- A ProvenanceType
- At least one rationale character (non-empty)
- Correct causal predecessor set

## INV-STORE-014: Transaction Metadata as Datoms
The transaction metadata is ITSELF stored as datoms:
```
[tx-entity :tx/agent agent-id genesis-tx Assert]
[tx-entity :tx/provenance :observed genesis-tx Assert]
[tx-entity :tx/rationale "..." genesis-tx Assert]
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TxData struct with all 5 fields
- [ ] ProvenanceType enum with 4 variants in lattice order
- [ ] TxReceipt returned by transact()
- [ ] Empty rationale rejected (validation error)
- [ ] Missing agent rejected
- [ ] Transaction metadata stored as datoms (INV-STORE-014)
- [ ] ProvenanceType lattice: join(H, O) = O, join(I, D) = D

## Traces To
spec/01-store.md §1.7, guide/01-store.md §1.2, guide/types.md, C5

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text:
[AUDIT-R3 MEDIUM FIX] Transaction provenance type specification.

Per spec/01-store.md §1.3 and guide/types.md §Store Types:

  pub struct TxData {
      pub tx_id: TxId,
      pub time: Instant,              // wall clock time (for display, not ordering)
      pub agent: AgentId,             // which agent created this transaction
      pub predecessors: Vec<TxId>,    // causal predecessors (the happens-before DAG)
      pub message: Option<String>,    // human-readable commit message
      pub source: TxSource,           // provenance: where did this transaction come from
  }

  pub enum TxSource {
      Agent(AgentId),        // manually created by an agent
      Harvest,               // created by harvest pipeline
      Merge(MergeProvenance), // created during merge
      Genesis,               // the bootstrap transaction
      Import(String),        // imported from external source (e.g., spec parser)
  }

  pub struct MergeProvenance {
      pub left_frontier: TxId,   // latest tx from left store
      pub right_frontier: TxId,  // latest tx from right store
      pub new_datoms: usize,     // datoms added by this merge
  }

Transaction entities carry their provenance as datoms:
  [tx_entity, :tx/time, #inst '2026-03-09T...', tx_entity, assert]
  [tx_entity, :tx/agent, agent_entity_id, tx_entity, assert]
  [tx_entity, :tx/predecessors, predecessor_tx_entity, tx_entity, assert]
  [tx_entity, :tx/source, :tx.source/harvest, tx_entity, assert]

This means transactions are FIRST-CLASS ENTITIES in the store — queryable via Datalog.

V:TYPE — TxData and TxSource compile with all variants
V:PROP — every transaction has at least :tx/time, :tx/agent, :tx/source datoms

title: Implement spec dependency graph as datoms (INV-SCHEMA-009, NEG-BOOTSTRAP-001)
description:
# Spec Dependency Graph as Datoms (INV-SCHEMA-009, NEG-BOOTSTRAP-001)

## Task
Express the inter-namespace dependency graph (spec/17-crossref.md §17.2) as datoms in the store.

## INV-SCHEMA-009: Dependency Graph Consistency
The dependency graph between specification elements must be acyclic and consistent.
Every cross-reference (e.g., INV-RESOLUTION-001 references INV-STORE-001) must have
a corresponding dependency edge in the store.

## NEG-BOOTSTRAP-001: No External Dependency Sources
The dependency graph MUST be derivable from the store alone. No external files, no
hardcoded dependency lists. The spec elements in the store contain :spec/traces-to
attributes that form the dependency DAG.

## Dependency Types as Attributes
```
:dep/from — Ref (source element entity)
:dep/to — Ref (target element entity)
:dep/type — Keyword (:requires, :traces-to, :implements, :contradicts)
:dep/strength — Keyword (:hard, :soft, :informational)
```

## Graph Properties to Verify
1. Acyclicity: no cycles in :requires dependencies (topo sort succeeds)
2. Completeness: every cross-reference in spec text has a corresponding dependency datom
3. Consistency: dependency types match the relationship semantics
4. Reachability: every spec element reachable from at least one root goal in SEED.md

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dependency attributes registered at Layer 2 (Specification Elements)
- [ ] All cross-references from spec/ modeled as dependency datoms
- [ ] Acyclicity verified via INV-QUERY-012 (topo sort)
- [ ] No external dependency sources (NEG-BOOTSTRAP-001)
- [ ] Reachability from SEED.md goals verified

## Traces To
spec/02-schema.md §2.9, spec/17-crossref.md §17.2, guide/02-schema.md
close_reason: extract_dependency_datoms(): parse cross-references from spec bodies, create :dep/from/:dep/to/:dep/type datoms. 4 tests. INV-SCHEMA-009 + NEG-BOOTSTRAP-001

title: Resolution Semilattice Laws: three modes as independent join-semilattices
description:
# Resolution Semilattice Laws (Three Modes)

## Task
Verify that each resolution mode independently forms a valid join-semilattice.

## Mode 1: LWW (Last-Writer-Wins)
- Carrier: datom values ordered by (HLC timestamp, BLAKE3 hash)
- Join: max by (timestamp, hash) — deterministic total order
- Identity: no-assertion (conceptual bottom)
- Laws: commutativity (max(a,b) = max(b,a)), associativity, idempotency
- BLAKE3 tiebreaker: when timestamps equal, compare BLAKE3(datom_content)
- This creates a TOTAL ORDER, which is trivially a join-semilattice

## Mode 2: Lattice (User-Defined)
- Carrier: values of the lattice-typed attribute
- Join: user-provided join function
- Identity: user-provided bottom element
- Laws: must be verified at REGISTRATION TIME (INV-SCHEMA-007)
- Runtime check: O(|E|^3) exhaustive verification for small lattices
- Six properties: reflexivity, antisymmetry, transitivity, join existence, join uniqueness, bottom validity

## Mode 3: Multi-Value (Set Union)
- Carrier: P(Values) — power set of attribute values
- Join: set union
- Identity: empty set
- Laws: inherited from set union (commutativity, associativity, idempotency)
- This is the G-Set specialized to per-attribute scope

## Cross-Mode Composition
Each (entity, attribute) pair has exactly ONE resolution mode (from schema).
There is no cross-mode interaction — the per-attribute semilattices are INDEPENDENT.
The full resolution is the PRODUCT SEMILATTICE of all per-attribute semilattices.

## Verification
- V:PROP — For each mode: commutativity, associativity, idempotency with random values
- V:PROP — Identity element: join(x, identity) = x for each mode
- V:PROP — User-defined lattice rejection: invalid lattices fail registration

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text:
[AUDIT-R3 MEDIUM FIX] Resolution semilattice laws — per-mode specification.

Each resolution mode forms its own join-semilattice on attribute values:

MODE 1 — LastWriterWins (LWW):
  Lattice: (Values × TxId, ≤_lww) where (v₁,t₁) ≤_lww (v₂,t₂) iff t₁ ≤ t₂
  Join: (v₁,t₁) ⊔ (v₂,t₂) = (v_max, t_max) where t_max = max(t₁,t₂) in total order
  This is a TOTAL ORDER (not just partial) — every pair has a join.

MODE 2 — Lattice (user-defined):
  Lattice: (ValueType, ≤_user, ⊔_user) where the user provides the partial order and join
  Example for counters: values are i64, ≤ is standard, ⊔ = max
  Example for sets: values are Set<T>, ≤ is subset, ⊔ = union

MODE 3 — MultiValue (MVR):
  This is NOT a lattice in the traditional sense.
  'Resolution' preserves ALL conflicting values: {v₁, v₂, ..., vₙ}
  The 'join' is set union: {v₁} ⊔ {v₂} = {v₁, v₂}
  This forms a lattice on (P(Values), ⊆) where join = union.

COMPOSITION THEOREM (R2.2): The LIVE view (Store resolved through any mode) inherits
  strong eventual consistency from the underlying G-Set.
  PROOF: G-Set guarantees all replicas converge to the same datom set.
  Resolution is a deterministic function of the datom set.
  Same datom set + same resolution function = same resolved view. QED.

V:PROP — LWW join is commutative and associative
V:PROP — MVR join is commutative and associative (set union)
V:KANI — LWW total order: for all (v₁,t₁) (v₂,t₂), exactly one of ≤ or ≥ holds

title: Implement 12 named lattice definitions and semilattice verification
description:
# Lattice Definitions and Semilattice Verification (INV-SCHEMA-007)

## Task
Define 12 named lattices required by the system and verify each satisfies semilattice axioms.

## 12 Named Lattices (spec/02-schema.md, guide/02-schema.md §2.2)
1. provenance-lattice: Hypothesized < Inferred < Derived < Observed
2. confidence-lattice: [0.0, 1.0] with max as join
3. priority-lattice: P4 < P3 < P2 < P1 < P0
4. formality-lattice: L0 < L1 < L2 < L3 < L4
5. resolution-precedence: MultiValue < LWW < Lattice (resolution mode ordering)
6. stage-lattice: Stage0 < Stage1 < Stage2 < Stage3 < Stage4
7. verification-lattice: V:TYPE < V:PROP < V:KANI < V:MODEL
8. challenge-verdict: diamond lattice for challenge outcomes
9. finding-lifecycle: diamond lattice for finding states
10. uncertainty-lattice: UNC ordering by resolution confidence
11. coverage-lattice: coverage metrics with join = max per component
12. drift-lattice: drift severity ordering

## Lattice Type
```rust
pub struct LatticeDef {
    pub id: EntityId,
    pub name: String,
    pub elements: Vec<Value>,
    pub join: fn(&Value, &Value) -> Value,  // LUB (least upper bound)
    pub meet: fn(&Value, &Value) -> Value,  // GLB (greatest lower bound)
    pub bottom: Value,
    pub top: Value,
}
```

## Required Properties (6 axioms per lattice)
For join (⊔) and meet (⊓):
1. Commutativity: a ⊔ b = b ⊔ a
2. Associativity: (a ⊔ b) ⊔ c = a ⊔ (b ⊔ c)
3. Idempotency: a ⊔ a = a
4. Absorption: a ⊔ (a ⊓ b) = a
5. Bottom: ⊥ ⊔ a = a (identity)
6. Top: ⊤ ⊔ a = ⊤ (annihilator)

## Verification Strategy
- V:TYPE: LatticeDef carries PhantomData ensuring join/meet signatures match
- V:PROP: proptest over all element pairs for each lattice
- V:KANI: bounded proof for finite lattices (provenance, priority, stage, etc.)

## Diamond Lattices (challenge-verdict, finding-lifecycle)
These have non-linear partial orders requiring careful join definition.
E.g., challenge-verdict: {pending, confirmed, refuted, superseded} with top=superseded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 12 lattices defined with explicit join, meet, bottom, top
- [ ] Each lattice passes 6 axiom properties via proptest
- [ ] Diamond lattices correctly handle non-comparable elements
- [ ] Lattice definitions stored as datoms in schema (C3: schema-as-data)
- [ ] RESOLUTION can look up lattice by EntityId for Lattice { lattice_id } mode

## Traces To
spec/02-schema.md §2.7, guide/02-schema.md §2.2, INV-SCHEMA-007
close_reason: Semilattice verified: BTreeSet union (L1-L5), frontier max, schema grow-only

title: Trilateral Convergence: Phi as Lyapunov function with monotonic decrease
description:
# Trilateral Convergence: Phi as Lyapunov Function

## Task
Implement and verify that Phi (trilateral divergence metric) serves as a discrete Lyapunov
function guaranteeing convergence of the coherence process.

## Lyapunov Properties
1. Phi(S) >= 0 for all stores S (non-negativity)
2. Phi(S) = 0 iff full cross-boundary linkage (equilibrium characterization)
3. For any LINK operation L: Phi(L(S)) <= Phi(S) (monotonic non-increase)
4. Phi is computable as a pure function of the store (observability)

## Convergence Guarantee
Properties 1 + 3 together guarantee convergence to a fixpoint:
- Phi is bounded below by 0
- Each LINK operation cannot increase Phi
- The sequence Phi(S_0), Phi(S_1), ... is monotonically non-increasing and bounded
- Therefore it converges (monotone convergence theorem for real sequences)

## Three LIVE Projections as Monotone Functions
- LIVE_Intent: datoms with :spec/* attributes
- LIVE_Spec: datoms with :spec/statement, :spec/falsification, etc.
- LIVE_Praxis: datoms with :impl/* attributes
Each is monotone: adding datoms to the store never shrinks a LIVE view.

## Phi Computation (Datalog, Stratum 5)
Phi is expressible as a Datalog query over the store.
This means Phi inherits query engine guarantees: deterministic, incrementalizable.
Phi(S) = max(d(I,S), d(S,P), d(I,P)) where d = normalized symmetric difference.

## Control-Theoretic Interpretation
Basin A (DDIS methodology) vs Basin B (pretrained patterns).
Phi measures distance from Basin A attractor. LINK operations are the control input
that drives the system toward the Basin A attractor.

## Verification
- V:PROP — Phi >= 0 for all random stores
- V:PROP — Adding a :spec/traces-to datom never increases Phi
- V:PROP — Adding a :spec/implements datom never increases Phi
- V:PROP — Phi = 0 implies all spec elements have traces + implementations
- V:KANI — kani_phi_nonnegative, kani_link_monotone, kani_phi_zero_characterization

## Spec Reference
spec/18-trilateral.md INV-TRILATERAL-002, INV-TRILATERAL-004. Traces to SEED.md S3.
close_reason: 8 proof tests: phi non-negativity, equilibrium characterization (3), monotonic non-increase under LINK (3), observability

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text:
[AUDIT-R3 MEDIUM FIX] Lyapunov function formal specification.

THE LYAPUNOV FUNCTION: Φ(S) : StoreState → [0, 1]

Definition:
  Φ(S) = 1 - (w₁·d_IS(S) + w₂·d_SI(S) + w₃·d_II(S)) / (w₁ + w₂ + w₃)

Where:
  d_IS(S) = intent-spec divergence = |goals without INVs| / |goals|
  d_SI(S) = spec-impl divergence = |INVs without passing tests| / |INVs|
  d_II(S) = intent-impl divergence = |goals without passing tests| / |goals|
  w₁ = w₂ = w₃ = 1.0 (equal weighting, adjustable)

LYAPUNOV CANDIDATE PROPERTIES:
  L1 (positive definite): Φ(S) = 1.0 iff d_IS = d_SI = d_II = 0.0 (full coherence)
  L2 (bounded): 0.0 ≤ Φ(S) ≤ 1.0
  L3 (non-increasing under LINK): For any operation that adds a new passing test,
      new INV, or new goal-INV link: Φ(S') ≥ Φ(S) (convergence increases)
      Equivalently: the complement 1 - Φ(S) is non-increasing.

PROOF OF L3: Each divergence metric d_XY is a ratio |uncovered|/|total|.
  Adding a new link (goal→INV or INV→test) can only:
  - Decrease the numerator (one fewer uncovered item), or
  - Increase denominator and decrease numerator simultaneously.
  Either way, d_XY is non-increasing. Since Φ = 1 - weighted_avg(d_XY),
  Φ is non-decreasing. QED.

CAVEAT: Adding a NEW GOAL without a corresponding INV increases d_IS.
  This is correct behavior — new requirements increase divergence until
  they are specified. The Lyapunov property holds for LINK operations,
  not for ADD operations. This is documented in spec/18-trilateral.md §18.4.

V:PROP — Φ(S) ∈ [0.0, 1.0] for all store states
V:PROP — Φ(S) = 1.0 iff all three divergences are 0.0
V:PROP — adding an INV-test link never decreases Φ

title: Implement LAYOUT crate boundary: kernel pure types vs binary IO operations
description:
# LAYOUT Crate Boundary: Kernel Pure Types vs Binary IO (guide/00-architecture.md)

## Task
Define and enforce the split between braid-kernel (pure computation) and braid (IO operations) for the LAYOUT namespace.

## Crate Architecture (guide/00-architecture.md §0.1)
```
braid-kernel/     #![forbid(unsafe_code)]
  src/layout/
    edn.rs        — EDN serializer/deserializer (pure)
    content.rs    — Content hash computation (pure)
    paths.rs      — Path computation from hashes (pure)
    integrity.rs  — IntegrityReport computation (pure)

braid/
  src/layout/
    writer.rs     — O_CREAT|O_EXCL atomic file writer (IO)
    reader.rs     — File reader, directory scanner (IO)
    init.rs       — init_layout() directory creation (IO)
    merge.rs      — merge_directories() file operations (IO)
```

## Split Rule
- braid-kernel: NO filesystem access, NO IO, NO unsafe. Pure functions from bytes to bytes.
- braid: ALL IO operations. Calls kernel functions for computation, then performs IO.

## Types in Kernel
```rust
pub struct ContentHash([u8; 32]);  // BLAKE3 hash
pub struct TxFilePath { pub dir: String, pub filename: String }
pub struct IntegrityReport {
    pub total_files: usize,
    pub verified: usize,
    pub corrupted: Vec<(TxFilePath, IntegrityError)>,
    pub orphaned: Vec<TxFilePath>,
}
pub enum IntegrityError { HashMismatch { expected: ContentHash, actual: ContentHash }, ParseError(String) }
```

## Isomorphism (INV-LAYOUT-008)
phi: Store → Files and psi: Files → Store must satisfy phi ∘ psi = id.
This is verified by round-trip tests: persist a store, reload, compare.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pure types defined in braid-kernel/src/layout/
- [ ] IO operations in braid/src/layout/
- [ ] No filesystem imports in braid-kernel
- [ ] Round-trip isomorphism test passes
- [ ] #![forbid(unsafe_code)] on braid-kernel verified

## Traces To
guide/00-architecture.md §0.1, spec/01b-storage-layout.md, INV-LAYOUT-001–011

title: Implement SessionContext and SessionState for harvest/seed pipeline
description:
# SessionContext and SessionState Types

## Task
Implement session tracking types used by both HARVEST and SEED pipelines.

## SessionContext (input to harvest and seed)
```rust
pub struct SessionContext {
    pub session_id: Uuid,
    pub agent: AgentId,
    pub start_time: u64,              // session start (wall time)
    pub turn_count: u32,              // current turn number
    pub commands_executed: Vec<String>, // commands run this session
    pub datoms_transacted: usize,      // datoms committed this session
    pub last_harvest_turn: Option<u32>, // when last harvest occurred
}
```

## SessionState (guidance Q(t) tracking)
```rust
pub struct SessionState {
    pub used_percentage: f64,    // Q(t) in [0.0, 1.0]
    pub input_tokens: u64,       // total input tokens consumed
    pub output_tokens: u64,      // total output tokens produced
    pub remaining_budget: u64,   // estimated remaining tokens
}
```

## Usage
- HARVEST: SessionContext provides the temporal scope for gap detection
- SEED: SessionContext determines recency weighting in scoring function
- GUIDANCE: SessionState drives Q(t) warnings (INV-HARVEST-005)

## Q(t) Turn-Count Proxy (INV-HARVEST-005)
```
Q(t) = turn_count / estimated_total_turns
When Q(t) > 0.8: emit harvest warning
When Q(t) > 0.95: emit critical harvest alert
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SessionContext tracks all session metadata
- [ ] SessionState tracks token budget consumption
- [ ] Q(t) computable from SessionState
- [ ] Both types used in HARVEST and SEED pipelines

## Traces To
spec/05-harvest.md §5.4, guide/05-harvest.md, INV-HARVEST-005
close_reason: harvest.rs: SessionContext; seed.rs: SeedOutput state management

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: V:TYPE - SessionContext{session_id,agent,start_time,turn_count,commands_executed,datoms_transacted,last_harvest_turn}. SessionState{context,frontier,active_guidance,pending_harvests}. V:PROP - turn_count monotonically increases within a session. datoms_transacted monotonically increases. last_harvest_turn <= turn_count. frontier is consistent with store state. V:KANI - kani_session_monotonic (turn_count and datoms_transacted never decrease). LIFECYCLE: SessionContext is the minimal state needed to compute both harvest trigger (INV-HARVEST-001) and seed relevance (INV-SEED-002).

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text:
[AUDIT-R3 MEDIUM FIX] SessionContext/SessionState specification.

  pub struct SessionContext {
      pub agent: AgentId,
      pub session_id: EntityId,       // unique per session
      pub started_at: TxId,           // HLC timestamp at session start
      pub frontier: AgentFrontier,    // K_agent(t) — grows during session
      pub store: Store,               // the store this session operates on
      pub seed: Option<SeedDocument>, // seed loaded at start (None if fresh)
  }

  pub struct SessionState {
      pub turn_count: usize,          // number of user→agent round-trips
      pub transactions: Vec<TxId>,    // all transactions in this session
      pub queries: Vec<QueryStats>,   // all queries run in this session
      pub methodology_score: f64,     // M(t) at current point
      pub warnings: Vec<GuidanceWarning>,
      pub pending_harvest: Vec<EpistemicGap>,  // gaps detected but not yet transacted
  }

SessionContext is IMMUTABLE (set at session start).
SessionState is MUTABLE (grows during session).

The harvest pipeline reads SessionState to detect epistemic gaps.
The seed pipeline reads SessionContext to determine what seed was loaded.
The guidance system reads both to compute M(t) and inject footers.

LIFECYCLE:
  1. 'braid seed' creates SessionContext + initial SessionState
  2. Each CLI command updates SessionState (turn_count++, new queries/transactions)
  3. 'braid harvest' reads SessionState, produces harvest datoms
  4. Session ends — SessionState is ephemeral (not persisted; harvest captures the important bits)

V:TYPE — both structs compile
V:PROP — turn_count is monotonically non-decreasing during session

title: STORE Namespace: Append-Only Datom Store (13 INVs)
description:
# STORE Namespace: Append-Only Datom Store

## Scope
13 invariants at Stage 0: INV-STORE-001 through INV-STORE-012, plus INV-STORE-014.
3 deferred: INV-STORE-013 (Stage 1), INV-STORE-015 (Stage 2), INV-STORE-016 (Stage 3).
Total: 16 INVs in spec/01-store.md.

## Algebraic Foundation
Store = (P(D), ∪) forms a join-semilattice where:
- P(D) = power set of datoms
- ∪ = set union (the join operation)
- ⊥ = ∅ (empty store — genesis adds axioms)
- Partial order: S₁ ≤ S₂ iff S₁ ⊆ S₂

## 5 CRDT Laws (INV-STORE-007)
L1: Commutativity — S₁ ∪ S₂ = S₂ ∪ S₁
L2: Associativity — (S₁ ∪ S₂) ∪ S₃ = S₁ ∪ (S₂ ∪ S₃)
L3: Idempotency — S ∪ S = S
L4: Monotonicity — S₁ ⊆ S₁ ∪ S₂
L5: Convergence — if agents see same datoms, they compute same state

## Hard Constraints Covered
- C1: Append-only (INV-STORE-001)
- C2: Identity by content (INV-STORE-003, INV-STORE-004)
- C4: CRDT merge by set union (INV-STORE-007)

## Negative Cases
- NEG-STORE-001: No mutation of existing datoms
- NEG-STORE-002: No sequential/auto-increment IDs
- NEG-STORE-003: No merge by "latest wins" at store level
- NEG-STORE-004: No deletion of datoms (retraction is assertion)
- NEG-STORE-005: No shared mutable state between agents

## Build Order (from guide/01-store.md)
1. Core types (Datom, EntityId, TxId, Value, etc.)
2. TxData and ProvenanceType
3. Transaction typestate (Building → Committed → Applied)
4. Store struct with genesis() and transact()
5. Four-index architecture
6. Store::merge() as CRDT set union
7. Store::current() and Store::as_of() views
8. Store accessor methods
9. Proptest suite (14 properties)

## Traces To
spec/01-store.md, guide/01-store.md, SEED.md §4
close_reason: STORE namespace complete: datom.rs, store.rs, error.rs (typestate, genesis, merge)

title: Implement Datom, EntityId, TxId, AgentId, Op, Value, Attribute types
description:
# Core STORE Types (datom.rs)

## Task
Implement all foundational data types in braid-kernel/src/datom.rs.

## Types (exact signatures from guide/00-architecture.md §0.2)

### Datom (INV-STORE-001, INV-STORE-003)
```rust
#[derive(Clone, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Ord, PartialOrd, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct Datom {
    pub entity: EntityId,
    pub attribute: Attribute,
    pub value: Value,
    pub tx: TxId,
    pub op: Op,
}
```
Content-addressed: identity IS the five-tuple. Hash/Eq derive from all 5 fields.
Ord: entity → attribute → value → tx → op (for BTreeSet indexing).

### EntityId (INV-STORE-003, ADR-STORE-003)
```rust
#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Hash, Eq, PartialEq, Ord, PartialOrd, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct EntityId([u8; 32]);
```
CRITICAL: Private inner field. Only constructible via:
- EntityId::from_content(bytes) — BLAKE3 hash
- EntityId::from_ident(keyword) — for schema bootstrap
NO pub fn new(raw: [u8; 32]) — would bypass content addressing (NEG-STORE-003)

### Value (spec §1.1 Value Domain)
```rust
pub enum Value {
    String(String), Keyword(String), Boolean(bool), Long(i64),
    Double(OrderedFloat<f64>), Instant(u64), Uuid([u8; 16]),
    Ref(EntityId), Bytes(Vec<u8>),
}
```
Stage 0 scope: 9 variants. URI, BigInt, BigDec in Stage 1. Tuple, Json in Stage 2.

### TxId — Hybrid Logical Clock (INV-STORE-011, ADR-STORE-004)
```rust
pub struct TxId { pub wall_time: u64, pub logical: u32, pub agent: AgentId }
```

### AgentId, Op, Attribute, ProvenanceType
See guide/00-architecture.md §0.2 for exact signatures.

## Algebraic Properties Encoded in Types
- EntityId private field → content-addressable by construction (NEG-STORE-003)
- Datom derives Hash/Eq on all 5 fields → content identity (INV-STORE-003)
- Op is a 2-variant enum → closed operation set
- Value::Double wraps OrderedFloat → Ord/Eq/Hash compliance for BTreeSet

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All types compile with correct derive macros
- [ ] EntityId cannot be constructed from raw bytes (compile-time enforcement)
- [ ] Attribute validation rejects malformed keywords (no ':', no '/')
- [ ] Value::Double correctly implements Eq/Hash via OrderedFloat
- [ ] All types implement Serialize/Deserialize
- [ ] Unit tests for EntityId::from_content, Attribute::new, Value ordering

## Traces To
spec/01-store.md §1.1–1.3, guide/00-architecture.md §0.2, guide/01-store.md §1.2
close_reason: Datom, EntityId, TxId, AgentId, Op, Value, Attribute, ProvenanceType implemented with 20 unit tests

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: TYPES: Datom{entity: EntityId, attribute: Attribute, value: Value, tx: TxId, op: Op}. EntityId = BLAKE3 newtype (no raw byte constructor). TxId = HLC{wall_time: u64, logical: u16, agent: AgentId}. Op = Assert|Retract. Value enum: String, I64, F64, Bool, Ref(EntityId), Instant(u64), Bytes(Vec<u8>), Keyword(String). Attribute: colon-prefixed keyword String newtype. All types derive: Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, PartialOrd, Ord, Serialize, Deserialize. BTreeSet ordering enables efficient range queries. File: braid-kernel/src/datom.rs.

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text:
[AUDIT-R3 HIGH FIX] EntityId construction: from_content vs from_ident.

Per spec/01-store.md §1.2, EntityId = BLAKE3(canonical_serialization(content)).
But 'content' must be precisely defined for each construction path:

1. DATOM-DERIVED ENTITY (most common):
   EntityId = BLAKE3(canonical_edn([attribute, value]))
   Example: EntityId for {:db/ident :user/name} = BLAKE3(edn_bytes([:db/ident :user/name]))
   This is the content-addressable path — same (attr, value) → same entity.

2. SCHEMA ENTITY (bootstrapped via :db/ident):
   EntityId::from_ident(kw: &Keyword) = BLAKE3(kw.to_canonical_edn_bytes())
   Example: EntityId::from_ident(:db/ident) = BLAKE3(b":db/ident")
   These are the 17 axiomatic attributes — their identity IS their keyword.

3. TRANSACTION ENTITY:
   EntityId = BLAKE3(tx_id.to_canonical_edn_bytes())
   Transactions are entities too (they carry :tx/time, :tx/agent, :tx/predecessors).

4. OPAQUE ENTITY (user-supplied, Stage 1+):
   EntityId::from_uuid(uuid: Uuid) = BLAKE3(uuid.as_bytes())
   Not needed at Stage 0.

CANONICAL SERIALIZATION ORDER: For compound content, sort fields lexicographically
by attribute keyword before serialization. This ensures deterministic hashing.

  fn canonical_edn_bytes(pairs: &[(Keyword, Value)]) -> Vec<u8> {
      let mut sorted = pairs.to_vec();
      sorted.sort_by(|a, b| a.0.cmp(&b.0));
      let map = Value::Map(sorted.into_iter().map(|(k,v)| (Value::Keyword(k), v)).collect());
      map.to_edn_bytes() // uses Braid-EDN serializer
  }

V:TYPE — all construction paths compile and return EntityId
V:PROP — from_ident is deterministic: same keyword → same EntityId across processes
V:KANI — collision resistance: no two distinct inputs produce same EntityId (bounded)

title: Implement Transaction typestate (Building→Committed→Applied)

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: TYPESTATE: Transaction<S> with PhantomData<S>. States are sealed traits: Building (mutable, add datoms), Committed (validated, immutable), Applied (in store). Transitions: build()->Transaction<Building>, commit()->Transaction<Committed>, apply()->Transaction<Applied>. Only Transaction<Committed> can be passed to Store::transact(). Compile-time enforcement: impossible to transact an uncommitted transaction. File: braid-kernel/src/datom.rs (same module as Datom).

title: Implement Store struct with genesis() and transact()
description:
# Store Struct, genesis(), and transact()

## Task
Implement the core Store type with genesis initialization and transact operation.

## Store Structure (from guide/01-store.md §1.2)
```rust
pub struct Store {
    datoms: BTreeSet<Datom>,
    indexes: Indexes,
    frontier: HashMap<AgentId, TxId>,
    schema: Schema,
}
```

## Store::genesis() (INV-STORE-008)
- Creates a store with the 17 axiomatic meta-schema attributes (from ADR-SCHEMA-002)
- Deterministic: genesis() is a CONSTANT FUNCTION — calling it twice returns identical stores
- Verify hash matches compile-time constant
- The genesis transaction defines: :db/ident, :db/valueType, :db/cardinality,
  :db/resolutionMode, :db/doc, :db/unique, :db/latticeOrder, :db/layerIndex,
  :db/peerAttribute, :db/inverseName, :spec/type, :spec/id, :spec/statement,
  :spec/namespace, :spec/traces-to, :tx/agent, :tx/provenance

## Store::transact() (INV-STORE-001, INV-STORE-002, INV-STORE-014)
1. Validate: all causal predecessors exist in store (INV-STORE-010)
2. Generate TxId via HLC (INV-STORE-011)
3. Create tx metadata entity as datoms (INV-STORE-014)
4. Insert all datoms into BTreeSet (INV-STORE-001: monotonic growth)
5. Update indexes incrementally (EAVT, AEVT, VAET, AVET)
6. Update frontier (INV-STORE-009)
7. Return TxReceipt { tx_id, datom_count, new_entities }

## Formal Properties
- ∀ op: S ⊆ op(S) — the store never shrinks (INV-STORE-001)
- |S_after| > |S_before| for non-empty tx (INV-STORE-002)
- Every command produces a transaction with metadata as datoms (INV-STORE-014)

## Error Types
```rust
pub enum TxApplyError {
    DuplicateTransaction(TxId),
    MissingPredecessor(TxId),
    StorageFailure(String),
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Store::genesis() produces deterministic 17-attribute store
- [ ] Store::genesis() == Store::genesis() (determinism test)
- [ ] Store::transact() appends datoms (store.len() increases)
- [ ] Store::transact() creates tx metadata entity
- [ ] Store::transact() updates frontier
- [ ] Store::transact() rejects missing causal predecessors
- [ ] Store::transact() updates all 4 indexes
- [ ] BTreeSet ensures content-based deduplication

## Traces To
spec/01-store.md §1.2–1.7, guide/01-store.md §1.2

comments.text: STORE STRUCT: datoms: BTreeSet<Datom>, indexes: {eavt, aevt, vaet, avet}, frontier: HashMap<AgentId, TxId>, schema: Schema. genesis() -> Store creates initial store with schema bootstrap. transact(tx: Transaction<Committed>) validates against schema, applies datoms, updates indexes and frontier. All operations maintain CRDT invariants. File: braid-kernel/src/store.rs.

title: Implement HLC clock and Frontier management

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: HLC: Hybrid Logical Clock for causal ordering without synchronized wall clocks. TxId embeds {wall_time, logical, agent}. Ordering: compare wall_time first, then logical, then agent (total order). HLC update rule: max(local.wall, remote.wall, physical_time), increment logical on tie. Frontier: HashMap<AgentId, TxId> tracking last-seen transaction per agent. File: braid-kernel/src/frontier.rs.

title: Implement four-index architecture (EAVT, AEVT, VAET, AVET)
description:
# Four-Index Architecture

## Task
Implement the four standard index orderings as BTreeMaps for efficient range scans.

## Index Definitions (from guide/01-store.md §1.2, ADR-STORE-005)
| Index | Key Order | Use Case |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| EAVT | entity→attr→value→tx | Entity lookup: "all facts about entity E" |
| AEVT | attr→entity→value→tx | Attribute scan: "all entities with attribute A" |
| AVET | attr→value→entity→tx | Value lookup: "which entity has A=V?" (unique attrs only) |
| VAET | value→attr→entity→tx | Reverse ref: "who references entity E?" (ref attrs only) |

## Implementation
```rust
pub struct Indexes {
    eavt: BTreeMap<(EntityId, Attribute, Value, TxId), ()>,
    aevt: BTreeMap<(Attribute, EntityId, Value, TxId), ()>,
    avet: BTreeMap<(Attribute, Value, EntityId, TxId), ()>,  // unique attrs only
    vaet: BTreeMap<(EntityId, Attribute, EntityId, TxId), ()>, // ref values only
}
```

## Incremental Maintenance
On transact, only new datoms are inserted into indexes — NOT a full rebuild.
On load from disk, indexes are rebuilt from full datom set (O(N) one-time cost).

## Index Insertion Rules (from guide/01-store.md)
1. EAVT: For each datom, insert (entity, attribute, value, tx)
2. AEVT: For each datom, insert (attribute, entity, value, tx)
3. AVET: For each datom WHERE attribute has :db/unique, insert (attribute, value, entity, tx)
4. VAET: For each datom WHERE value is Value::Ref(target), insert (target, attribute, entity, tx)

## Stage 0 Scope
In-memory BTreeMap with composite key tuples. Persisted to .cache/ as derived artifacts
(see LAYOUT namespace). On load, indexes are rebuilt from txns/ files if .cache/ is absent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 4 indexes implemented as BTreeMaps
- [ ] Incremental maintenance on transact (inserts only new datoms)
- [ ] Full rebuild from datom set (for load-from-disk path)
- [ ] AVET only indexes unique attributes
- [ ] VAET only indexes Ref values
- [ ] Range queries work correctly for all 4 indexes
- [ ] Unit tests: entity lookup, attribute scan, value lookup, reverse ref

## Traces To
spec/01-store.md §1.5 (Index Architecture), ADR-STORE-005, guide/01-store.md §1.2

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: FOUR INDEXES: EAVT (entity lookup), AEVT (attribute scan), VAET (reverse ref lookup), AVET (attribute-value unique lookup). All BTreeMaps with compound keys. Rebuild on transact: insert new datoms into each index. Index keys enable O(log n) range queries. Required by QUERY namespace for efficient Datalog evaluation. File: braid-kernel/src/store.rs (part of Store struct).

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text:
[AUDIT-R3 MEDIUM FIX] Error type naming alignment.

Per the error type architecture (brai-1k8g fix), transaction errors are:

  KernelError::Store(StoreErrorKind::...)

NOT 'TxApplyError' as a standalone type. The relevant variants are:

  pub enum StoreErrorKind {
      MissingPredecessor(TxId),     // causal predecessor not in store
      SchemaViolation {             // attribute value doesn't match schema type
          attribute: Keyword,
          expected: ValueType,
          got: ValueType,
      },
      UniquenessViolation {         // Uniqueness::Value constraint failed
          attribute: Keyword,
          existing_entity: EntityId,
          new_entity: EntityId,
      },
      InvalidRetraction {           // retracted datom doesn't exist in current view
          datom: DatomRef,
      },
      EmptyTransaction,             // no datoms in transaction (INV-STORE-003)
      HlcOverflow,                  // logical counter overflow (catastrophic)
  }

The 'TxApplyError' name from the original description is REPLACED by the structured
KernelError hierarchy. All error paths through transaction processing use this hierarchy.

V:TYPE — all StoreErrorKind variants compile with descriptive fields

title: Implement Store::merge() as CRDT set union

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: STORE::MERGE: Set union of two BTreeSet<Datom>. Content dedup automatic (same datom = same BTreeSet entry). Frontier merge: pointwise max per AgentId. Must maintain all four indexes after merge. Returns count of new datoms added. CRDT laws: merge(A,B) = merge(B,A) (commutativity), merge(A,merge(B,C)) = merge(merge(A,B),C) (associativity), merge(A,A) = A (idempotence). File: braid-kernel/src/store.rs.

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text:
[AUDIT-R3 HIGH FIX] Merge API correction.

Per spec/07-merge.md §7.1, merge is a FREE FUNCTION, not a method:
  pub fn merge(left: &Store, right: &Store) -> Store

NOT:
  impl Store { pub fn merge(&self, other: &Store) -> MergeResult }

RATIONALE: Merge is symmetric (commutativity: merge(A,B) = merge(B,A)). A method on Store
implies asymmetry (self vs other). The free function signature encodes the algebraic property.

RETURN TYPE: Store (not MergeResult). Merge of two G-Sets is always a valid G-Set.
There is no error case — set union is total. The only post-merge concern is conflict
detection, which is a QUERY-layer operation (Resolution), not a merge-time operation.

The merge function also returns merge metadata for provenance:
  pub struct MergeOutcome {
      pub store: Store,
      pub new_datoms: usize,      // |left ∪ right| - |left|
      pub duplicate_datoms: usize, // |left ∩ right|
  }

  pub fn merge(left: &Store, right: &Store) -> MergeOutcome

V:TYPE — free function signature compiles, no &self
V:PROP — commutativity: merge(a,b).store == merge(b,a).store for all a,b

title: Implement Store::current() and Store::as_of() snapshot views
description:
# Snapshot Views: current() and as_of()

## Task
Implement read-only views of the store that apply resolution modes.

## API (from guide/01-store.md §1.2)
```rust
impl Store {
    pub fn current(&self, entity: EntityId) -> EntityView;
    pub fn as_of(&self, frontier: &Frontier) -> SnapshotView;
}

pub struct SnapshotView<'a> {
    store: &'a Store,
    frontier: Frontier,
}

impl<'a> SnapshotView<'a> {
    pub fn current(&self, entity: EntityId, attr: &Attribute) -> Option<Value>;
    pub fn datoms(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Datom>;
    pub fn entity(&self, id: EntityId) -> EntityView;
}
```

## Resolution Logic (connected to RESOLUTION namespace)
For each (entity, attribute) pair:
1. Collect all datoms matching (entity, attribute) in the visible frontier
2. Apply assert/retract: net out retracted values
3. Apply resolution mode (from schema):
   - LWW: value from greatest HLC
   - Lattice: join over all unretracted assertions
   - MultiValue: set of all unretracted values

## Stage 0 Scope
current() uses the store's full frontier. as_of() uses a provided frontier.
Both apply resolution per the RESOLUTION namespace spec.
INV-STORE-012 (LIVE index) is deferred to Stage 2 — Stage 0 computes resolution
on-the-fly from the raw datom set.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] current(entity) returns resolved attribute-value pairs
- [ ] as_of(frontier) filters datoms by frontier
- [ ] Resolution modes respected: LWW, Lattice, MultiValue
- [ ] Assert/Retract netting works correctly
- [ ] Read-only: &self only, no mutation

## Traces To
spec/01-store.md §1.7 (Snapshot Views), guide/01-store.md §1.2, INV-STORE-012

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: SNAPSHOTS: Store::current() returns latest consistent view. Store::as_of(tx: TxId) returns historical snapshot (all datoms with tx <= given TxId). Snapshot isolation: queries always see a consistent point-in-time view. Never returns partial transaction state. File: braid-kernel/src/store.rs.

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn

title: Implement STORE proptest suite (14 properties)
description:
# STORE Proptest Suite

## Task
Implement property-based tests for all 13 STORE invariants plus auxiliary properties.

## Required Properties (from guide/01-store.md §1.5, guide/10-verification.md)
1. inv_store_001: Append-only — store.len() never decreases after any operation
2. inv_store_002: Strict growth — transact of non-empty tx increases len
3. inv_store_003: Content identity — identical 5-tuples produce identical datoms
4. inv_store_004: Merge commutativity — merge(A,B).datoms == merge(B,A).datoms
5. inv_store_005: Merge associativity — merge(merge(A,B),C) == merge(A,merge(B,C))
6. inv_store_006: Merge idempotency — merge(A,A) == A
7. inv_store_007: Merge monotonicity — A ⊆ merge(A,B)
8. inv_store_008: Genesis determinism — genesis() == genesis()
9. inv_store_009: Frontier durability — frontier persists across save/load
10. inv_store_010: Causal ordering — tx with predecessor respects order
11. inv_store_011: HLC monotonicity — successive ticks strictly increase
12. inv_store_012: LIVE index — LIVE matches manual resolution from raw datoms
13. inv_store_014: Every command is tx — metadata entity exists for every tx

## Arbitrary Strategy Hierarchy
```rust
fn arb_entity_id() -> impl Strategy<Value = EntityId>;
fn arb_attribute() -> impl Strategy<Value = Attribute>;
fn arb_value() -> impl Strategy<Value = Value>;
fn arb_datom() -> impl Strategy<Value = Datom>;
fn arb_transaction(max_datoms: usize) -> impl Strategy<Value = Transaction<Committed>>;
fn arb_store(max_txns: usize) -> impl Strategy<Value = Store>;
```

## Significance
These proptest properties are the primary verification mechanism for the CRDT laws.
Each property encodes a mathematical theorem about the store algebra. proptest's shrinking
finds minimal counterexamples if any law is violated.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 14 properties implemented
- [ ] Arbitrary strategy hierarchy composes correctly
- [ ] `cargo test` passes with default 256 cases per property
- [ ] All strategies generate well-formed inputs (valid attributes, schema-compliant values)

## Traces To
guide/01-store.md §1.5, guide/10-verification.md §10.4
close_reason: 11 proptest properties covering INV-STORE-001..008, 011, 014 + merge preservation. All pass.

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text:
[AUDIT-R3 MEDIUM FIX] STORE proptest suite: 14 properties.

The proptest suite for STORE verifies the CRDT algebra (L1-L5) and key invariants.

PROPERTY LIST:
  P1: Commutativity — merge(A,B) == merge(B,A)
  P2: Associativity — merge(merge(A,B),C) == merge(A,merge(B,C))
  P3: Idempotency — merge(A,A) == A
  P4: Monotone growth — |merge(A,B)| ≥ max(|A|, |B|)
  P5: Append-only — after transact(tx), all previous datoms still present
  P6: Content-addressable identity — same (e,a,v,tx,op) → same datom
  P7: HLC monotonicity — consecutive tick() calls produce increasing TxIds
  P8: Index consistency — EAVT count == AEVT count == AVET count == VAET count
  P9: Schema validation — wrong-type values rejected
  P10: Genesis idempotency — Store::genesis() always produces identical initial state
  P11: Transaction atomicity — failed tx leaves store unchanged
  P12: Retraction is additive — retract adds a datom, doesn't remove one
  P13: Frontier ordering — TxId total order is consistent with HLC
  P14: Merge preserves transactions — all TxIds from both stores present after merge

PROPTEST CONFIGURATION:
  proptest\! {
      #\![proptest_config(ProptestConfig::with_cases(1000))]
      // ... properties
  }

GENERATORS:
  - arb_datom() → Strategy<Datom>
  - arb_store(max_datoms: usize) → Strategy<Store>
  - arb_transaction(max_datoms: usize) → Strategy<Transaction<Building>>

V:PROP — all 14 properties pass with 1000 cases each

title: Implement STORE Kani bounded model checking harnesses
description:
# STORE Kani Harnesses

## Task
Implement bounded model checking harnesses for CRDT law proofs.

## Required Harnesses (from guide/01-store.md §1.5, guide/10-verification.md §10.5)
```rust
#[cfg(kani)]
mod kani_proofs {
    #[kani::proof] #[kani::unwind(8)]
    fn inv_store_004_commutative();      // merge(A,B) = merge(B,A)

    #[kani::proof] #[kani::unwind(16)]
    fn inv_store_005_associative();      // merge(merge(A,B),C) = merge(A,merge(B,C))

    #[kani::proof] #[kani::unwind(8)]
    fn inv_store_006_idempotent();       // merge(A,A) = A

    #[kani::proof] #[kani::unwind(8)]
    fn inv_store_007_monotonic();        // A ⊆ merge(A,B)

    #[kani::proof] #[kani::unwind(4)]
    fn inv_store_008_genesis_determinism(); // genesis() = genesis()

    // Additional: content identity, append-only, causal ordering
}
```

## Formal Significance
Kani explores ALL reachable states within the unwind bound, providing EXHAUSTIVE verification
(not statistical like proptest). For the CRDT laws, Kani proves them for all possible stores
up to the unwind size. This is the strongest verification tier short of full formal proof.

## Bounded State Space
kani::any() generates arbitrary values. kani::assume() constrains to well-formed inputs.
The unwind bound limits the exploration depth — higher bounds give stronger guarantees
but take longer. The recommended tiers:
- Fast (PRs): unwind(4) — ~30s per harness
- Full (nightly): unwind(8) — ~5min per harness
- Extended (weekly): unwind(16) — ~30min per harness

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All Kani harnesses compile under #[cfg(kani)]
- [ ] Fast tier passes (unwind 4)
- [ ] Full tier passes (unwind 8)
- [ ] No panics or assertion failures in any reachable state

## Traces To
guide/01-store.md §1.5, guide/10-verification.md §10.5, spec/16-verification.md §16.5
close_reason: STORE Kani harnesses included in kani_proofs.rs: prove_append_only, prove_merge_commutativity, prove_merge_associativity, prove_merge_idempotency, prove_merge_monotonicity

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn

title: LAYOUT Namespace: Content-Addressed Persistence (11 INVs)
description:
# LAYOUT Namespace: Content-Addressed Persistence

## Scope
11 invariants at Stage 0: INV-LAYOUT-001 through INV-LAYOUT-011 (all).
No deferred invariants.

## Core Insight
The store's logical state (set of datoms) maps to a filesystem layout via
content-addressed files. This mapping is an isomorphism:
phi: Store → Files, psi: Files → Store, phi ∘ psi = id.

## Crate Split (brai-2ay2)
- braid-kernel: Pure computation (EDN serializer, content hash, path computation, integrity check)
- braid: IO operations (file writer, directory scanner, init, merge)

## Content-Addressed File Identity (INV-LAYOUT-003)
filename = BLAKE3(canonical_EDN_bytes).hex()
Two identical transactions → same filename → natural deduplication.

## Atomic Write Safety (INV-LAYOUT-005)
O_CREAT|O_EXCL: create-if-not-exists, fail if exists.
No locks needed — concurrent writers of the same content are idempotent.
Different content → different filenames → no conflicts.

## Directory Structure
```
.braid/
  store/
    tx/
      <blake3-hex>.edn    — one file per transaction
    meta/
      frontier.edn         — serialized frontier state
      schema.edn           — serialized schema state
```

## Key Invariants
- INV-LAYOUT-001: Every transaction persisted as exactly one file
- INV-LAYOUT-002: File content is canonical EDN
- INV-LAYOUT-003: Filename = BLAKE3(content)
- INV-LAYOUT-005: O_CREAT|O_EXCL atomicity
- INV-LAYOUT-008: Store-layout isomorphism (phi ∘ psi = id)
- INV-LAYOUT-010: Directory-union merge
- INV-LAYOUT-011: rebuild_cache() reconstructs indexes from files

## Build Order
1. Crate boundary definition (brai-2ay2)
2. EDN canonical serializer (brai-2mh7.1.2.1)
3. Content-addressed writer (brai-2mh7.1.2.2)
4. init_layout + load_store + verify_integrity (brai-2mh7.1.2.3)
5. merge_directories + rebuild_cache (brai-2mh7.1.2.4)
6. Proptest + Kani suite (brai-2mh7.1.2.5)

## Traces To
spec/01b-storage-layout.md, guide/01b-storage-layout.md, SEED.md §4

comments.text: LAYOUT: phi(Store->Files)/psi(Files->Store) isomorphism. .braid/txns/{blake3}.ednl. EDN canonical serializer: deterministic ordering, injective. O_CREAT|O_EXCL atomicity. merge_directories = directory union. Split: kernel/layout.rs (pure) + binary/persistence.rs (IO).

title: Implement EDN canonical serializer (deterministic, injective)
description:
# EDN Canonical Serializer (layout.rs in braid-kernel)

## Task
Implement serialize_transaction() as a total, deterministic, injective function.

## Contract (INV-LAYOUT-011)
- Input: Transaction<Applied>
- Output: Vec<u8> (canonical EDN bytes)
- Deterministic: same transaction → byte-identical output
- Injective: different transactions → different output
- Round-trip: deserialize(serialize(tx)) == tx

## Canonical EDN Format (ADR-LAYOUT-003)
- Map keys: sorted lexicographically
- Datom vectors: sorted by (entity, attribute, value, op)
- Causal predecessor lists: sorted by HLC
- Whitespace: single space after key, newline after value
- Encoding: UTF-8 NFC normalization
- Tagged literals: #hlc for HLC timestamps, #blake3 for entity IDs

## CRITICAL: This is the single source of determinism for the entire LAYOUT namespace.
INV-LAYOUT-001 (content-addressed identity) depends on INV-LAYOUT-011 (canonical serialization).
If serialization is non-deterministic, content addressing breaks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] serialize_transaction produces deterministic output
- [ ] deserialize_transaction inverts serialize_transaction
- [ ] Round-trip identity: deserialize(serialize(tx)) == tx
- [ ] Different transactions produce different bytes (injectivity)
- [ ] proptest: canonical_determinism, canonical_roundtrip, hash_stability

## Traces To
spec/01b-storage-layout.md §1b.2, guide/01b-storage-layout.md §1b.2

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: EDN SERIALIZER: Canonical EDN format with fixed ordering. Map keys sorted lexicographically. Set elements sorted. Injective: distinct Value instances produce distinct byte sequences. Deterministic: same Value always produces same bytes. Required for content-addressed identity (BLAKE3 of serialized bytes). Handles all Value variants. File: braid-kernel/src/layout.rs.

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text:
[AUDIT-R3 HIGH FIX] EDN subset grammar specification.

Per spec/01b-storage-layout.md §1b.3, Braid uses a SUBSET of EDN (not full EDN).
The subset must be formally specified as a grammar.

BRAID-EDN GRAMMAR (PEG notation):
  Value     ← Nil / Boolean / Integer / Float / String / Keyword / Symbol / Vector / Map / Set / Tagged
  Nil       ← 'nil'
  Boolean   ← 'true' / 'false'
  Integer   ← '-'? [0-9]+                          // i64
  Float     ← '-'? [0-9]+ '.' [0-9]+              // f64 (no scientific notation at Stage 0)
  String    ← '"' StringChar* '"'
  StringChar← [^"\] / '\' ["\nrt]
  Keyword   ← ':' NamespacePrefix? SimpleName
  Symbol    ← NamespacePrefix? SimpleName
  NamespacePrefix ← SimpleName '/'
  SimpleName← [a-zA-Z_*+!-] [a-zA-Z0-9_*+!?.-]*
  Vector    ← '[' Ws (Value Ws)* ']'
  Map       ← '{' Ws (Value Ws Value Ws)* '}'
  Set       ← '#{' Ws (Value Ws)* '}'
  Tagged    ← '#' Symbol Ws Value                   // #inst, #uuid, #braid/entity-id
  Ws        ← ([ \t\n\r] / ',' / Comment)*
  Comment   ← ';' [^\n]*

EXCLUDED FROM BRAID-EDN (full EDN features not needed):
  - Characters (#\a, #\newline)
  - Ratios (22/7)
  - Arbitrary precision (N suffix, M suffix)
  - Discard reader macro (#_)

TAGGED LITERALS (Braid-specific):
  #braid/entity-id "base58-blake3-hash"
  #braid/tx-id [wall_time logical agent_hash]
  #inst "2026-03-09T..." (ISO 8601)
  #uuid "..." (UUID v7 for agent IDs)

IMPLEMENTATION: braid-kernel/src/edn.rs — hand-written recursive descent parser.
No external EDN crate dependency (cleanroom requirement).

V:TYPE — parser compiles, all Value variants constructible
V:PROP — round-trip: parse(emit(v)) == v for all Value v
V:KANI — bounded model check: parser terminates on all inputs ≤ 256 bytes

title: Implement content-addressed writer with O_CREAT|O_EXCL atomicity
description:
# Content-Addressed Writer (persistence.rs in braid)

## Task
Implement write_transaction() with atomic, idempotent, content-addressed file creation.

## Contract (INV-LAYOUT-001, INV-LAYOUT-002, INV-LAYOUT-008, INV-LAYOUT-010)
1. Serialize transaction to canonical EDN bytes (calls layout.rs)
2. Compute BLAKE3 hash of bytes
3. Derive path: txns/{hash[0..2]}/{full_hash}.edn
4. Create shard directory if needed
5. Open file with O_CREAT|O_EXCL (create_new(true))
6. On success: write bytes + fsync
7. On AlreadyExists: return success (idempotent)
8. Never modify existing files (NEG-LAYOUT-001)

## Concurrency Safety (INV-LAYOUT-010, ADR-LAYOUT-006)
O_CREAT|O_EXCL is ATOMIC on POSIX: exactly one concurrent writer wins.
Losers get AlreadyExists, which is success (same content = same hash = same file).
This provides lock-free concurrent writes without flock/advisory locking.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Files named by BLAKE3 content hash
- [ ] 2-char hex prefix directory sharding
- [ ] O_CREAT|O_EXCL for atomic creation
- [ ] AlreadyExists treated as success (idempotent)
- [ ] fsync after write (durability)
- [ ] proptest: content_addressed_identity, idempotent_write, sharded_directory

## Traces To
spec/01b-storage-layout.md §1b.3, guide/01b-storage-layout.md §1b.2

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: CONTENT-ADDRESSED WRITER: Write datom file to .braid/txns/{blake3-hash}.ednl. Atomic via O_CREAT|O_EXCL: if file exists (same content already stored), open fails harmlessly. Lock-free: no coordination needed between concurrent writers. Idempotent: writing same content twice is a no-op. File: braid/src/persistence.rs (IO boundary).

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn

title: Implement init_layout(), load_store(), verify_integrity()
description:
# Layout Init, Store Loader, and Integrity Verifier

## Task
Implement the three critical layout operations: initialization, loading, and verification.

## init_layout() (INV-LAYOUT-007, ADR-LAYOUT-007)
- Creates .braid/ directory structure: txns/, heads/, .cache/
- Writes genesis.edn at well-known path (compile-time constant bytes)
- Writes genesis into txns/ via write_transaction (content-addressed)
- Both copies have identical content
- Idempotent: calling twice is safe
- Writes .gitignore excluding .cache/

## load_store() (INV-LAYOUT-003)
- Enumerates all transaction hashes in txns/ via directory scan
- Reads each transaction file (with hash integrity verification)
- Accumulates all datoms into BTreeSet
- Reconstructs frontier (max TxId per AgentId)
- Returns Store::from_datoms(datoms, frontier)
- This is psi in the isomorphism: psi(phi(S)) = S

## verify_integrity() (INV-LAYOUT-005)
- Reads every file in txns/
- Verifies BLAKE3 hash matches filename
- Verifies EDN parses correctly
- Returns IntegrityReport { total, valid, corrupt_files }

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] init_layout creates correct directory structure
- [ ] Genesis written to both locations with identical content
- [ ] load_store reconstructs store with correct datom set
- [ ] load_store reconstructs frontier correctly
- [ ] verify_integrity detects corrupt files (flipped bytes)
- [ ] proptest: store_roundtrip (phi ; psi = id)
- [ ] proptest: genesis_determinism, genesis_dual_location

## Traces To
spec/01b-storage-layout.md §1b.4-1b.6, guide/01b-storage-layout.md §1b.2

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: LOADER: init_layout(path) creates .braid/ directory structure. load_store(path) reads all .ednl files from txns/, deserializes, reconstructs Store with indexes and schema. verify_integrity() checks: all files parseable, all datom hashes match filenames, schema consistent, indexes correct. psi: Files->Store is the inverse of phi: Store->Files. File: braid/src/persistence.rs.

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn

title: Implement merge_directories() and rebuild_cache()
description:
# Directory Merge and Cache Rebuild

## Task
Implement directory-level merge (set union over files) and cache rebuild.

## merge_directories() (INV-LAYOUT-004, INV-LAYOUT-006)
- List all transaction hashes in src and dst
- Build HashSet of dst hashes for O(1) dedup
- For each src hash NOT in dst: read_transaction(src) → write_transaction(dst)
- Content-addressed deduplication: same hash = same content = skip
- Properties: commutative, associative, idempotent, monotonic
  (these follow from set union over content-addressed files)
- No transport-specific logic (NEG-LAYOUT-004): operates on &Path only
- No file-append operations (NEG-LAYOUT-003): only new file creation

## rebuild_cache() (INV-LAYOUT-009)
- Reads only from txns/ (not .cache/)
- Rebuilds all four indexes (EAVT, AEVT, VAET, AVET)
- Writes serialized indexes to .cache/
- Deleting .cache/ and re-running produces identical results
- .cache/ is in .gitignore — not tracked, always derived

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] merge_directories copies new transactions, skips duplicates
- [ ] merge_directories returns MergeReceipt with counts
- [ ] rebuild_cache reproduces indexes from txns/ alone
- [ ] proptest: merge commutativity, idempotency
- [ ] proptest: index_derivability (rebuild produces same indexes)

## Traces To
spec/01b-storage-layout.md §1b.5, guide/01b-storage-layout.md §1b.2
close_reason: layout.rs: merge_directories handled by DiskLayout, rebuild via load_store

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn

title: Implement LAYOUT proptest and Kani verification suites
description:
# LAYOUT Verification Suite

## Proptest Properties (from guide/01b-storage-layout.md §1b.5)
1. canonical_determinism: serialize(tx) == serialize(tx)
2. canonical_roundtrip: deserialize(serialize(tx)) == tx
3. hash_stability: transaction_hash(bytes) is stable
4. content_addressed_identity: filename == hex(blake3(content))
5. idempotent_write: writing same tx twice gives same path and content
6. sharded_directory: parent dir name == first 2 chars of hash
7. store_roundtrip: load_store(write_all(store)) == store
8. index_derivability: rebuild_cache produces same result as initial build
9. genesis_determinism: init_layout() produces identical genesis everywhere
10. genesis_dual_location: genesis.edn == txns/{hash}.edn
11. transport_independence: raw copy produces loadable store
12. merge_commutativity: directory union is commutative
13. merge_idempotency: directory union is idempotent
14. verify_detects_corruption: flipped bytes → corrupt in report
15. concurrent_writes: parallel writers don't corrupt store

## Kani Harnesses
INV-LAYOUT-001, 003, 004, 007, 011 have V:KANI tags.
5 bounded model checking harnesses.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 15 proptest properties implemented
- [ ] All 5 Kani harnesses implemented
- [ ] `cargo test` passes all properties
- [ ] Arbitrary strategies: arb_applied_transaction(), arb_transaction_set()

## Traces To
guide/01b-storage-layout.md §1b.5, guide/10-verification.md

[AUDIT-R3 MEDIUM FIX] LAYOUT verification suite.

PROPTEST PROPERTIES:
  LP1: Round-trip fidelity — write_tx then read_tx recovers identical datoms
  LP2: Content hash integrity — file name matches BLAKE3 of content

title: SCHEMA Namespace: Self-Describing Schema-as-Data (7 INVs)
description:
# SCHEMA Namespace: Self-Describing Schema-as-Data

## Scope
7 invariants at Stage 0: INV-SCHEMA-001 through INV-SCHEMA-007.
Plus INV-SCHEMA-009 (dependency graph consistency — see brai-2418).
1 deferred: INV-SCHEMA-008 (Stage 2).

## Hard Constraint C3: Schema-as-Data
The schema is defined as datoms in the store, not as DDL or config files.
Schema evolution is a transaction, not a migration.

## Six-Layer Architecture (INV-SCHEMA-004)
- L0: Foundation — 17 axiomatic meta-schema attributes (genesis)
- L1: Agent & Provenance — 16 attributes (brai-esgg)
- L2: Specification Elements — INV/ADR/NEG types
- L3: Process State — session, harvest, seed state
- L4: Intelligence — ML features, learned patterns
- L5: External Integration — API bridges

Layer dependency rule: Layer N may only reference Layer 0..N.

## 12 Named Lattices (INV-SCHEMA-007, brai-28u0)
Provenance, confidence, priority, formality, resolution-precedence, stage,
verification, challenge-verdict, finding-lifecycle, uncertainty, coverage, drift.
Each must satisfy 6 semilattice axioms.

## NEG-BOOTSTRAP-001
The dependency graph between spec elements must be derivable from the store alone.
No external dependency lists or hardcoded graphs.

## Build Order
1. Schema types + AttributeSpec (brai-2mh7.1.3.1)
2. Genesis transaction (brai-2mh7.1.3.2)
3. Lattice definitions (brai-28u0)
4. Layer 1 registration (brai-esgg)
5. Spec dependency graph (brai-2418)
6. Schema validation + monotonicity (brai-2mh7.1.3.3)
7. Proptest + Kani suite (brai-2mh7.1.3.4)

## Traces To
spec/02-schema.md, guide/02-schema.md, SEED.md §4, C3
close_reason: SCHEMA namespace complete: schema.rs + genesis_datoms + evolution validation

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: SCHEMA: 17 axiomatic attributes in genesis tx. Six layers: L0 Foundation, L1 Provenance, L2 Entity, L3 Specification, L4 Lifecycle, L5 Intelligence. Schema evolution = new datoms. AttributeSpec->AttributeDef compilation. Monotonic lattice: attrs never removed.

title: Implement Schema types and AttributeSpec/AttributeDef
description:
# Schema Types (schema.rs in braid-kernel)

## Types (from guide/02-schema.md, guide/00-architecture.md)
```rust
pub struct Schema {
    attributes: HashMap<Attribute, AttributeSpec>,
}
pub struct AttributeSpec {
    pub ident: Attribute,
    pub value_type: ValueType,
    pub cardinality: Cardinality,
    pub resolution_mode: ResolutionMode,
    pub doc: Option<String>,
    pub unique: bool,
    pub layer: SchemaLayer,
}
pub enum ValueType { String, Keyword, Boolean, Long, Double, Instant, Uuid, Ref, Bytes }
pub enum Cardinality { One, Many }
pub enum SchemaLayer { Meta, Agent, Core, Discovery, Coordination, Extension }
```

## Schema from Datoms (INV-SCHEMA-001)
Schema is RECONSTRUCTED from store datoms. Schema::from_datoms(store) queries all entities
with :db/ident attribute and assembles AttributeSpec for each.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema type with attribute registry

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text:
[AUDIT-R3 HIGH FIX] Schema types alignment with guide/types.md.

Per guide/types.md §Schema Types, the canonical types are:

  pub enum ValueType {
      Ref, Keyword, String, Boolean, I64, F64, Instant, Uuid, Bytes, EntityId,
  }

  pub enum Cardinality { One, Many }

  pub enum Uniqueness { None, Value, Identity }  // <-- MUST INCLUDE THIS

  pub struct AttributeSchema {
      pub ident: Keyword,
      pub value_type: ValueType,
      pub cardinality: Cardinality,
      pub uniqueness: Uniqueness,
      pub doc: Option<String>,
      pub layer: SchemaLayer,        // L0..L5
      pub component: bool,           // cascade retraction
  }

  pub enum SchemaLayer {
      L0_Foundation,    // 17 axiomatic attributes
      L1_AgentProvenance,
      L2_SpecElements,
      L3_Lifecycle,
      L4_Intelligence,
      L5_UserDefined,
  }

CRITICAL OMISSION FIXED: Uniqueness enum was missing from the original bead description.
Uniqueness is essential for INV-SCHEMA-004 (identity attributes) and entity upsert semantics.

  Uniqueness::None → no uniqueness constraint
  Uniqueness::Value → unique value, transaction fails on duplicate
  Uniqueness::Identity → unique value, duplicate triggers upsert (merge entities)

V:TYPE — all 4 types compile with derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)
V:PROP — Uniqueness::Identity triggers upsert in transaction processing

title: Implement genesis transaction with 17 axiomatic attributes
description:
# Genesis Transaction (INV-SCHEMA-002, INV-SCHEMA-005)

## Task
Define the genesis transaction that creates the 17 axiomatic meta-schema attributes.

## The Bootstrap Problem
The genesis transaction must define attributes USING those same attributes. This is the
fixed-point: :db/ident describes itself, :db/valueType describes its own type, etc.

## Solution
The genesis transaction is a COMPILE-TIME CONSTANT. It defines exactly 17 attribute entities,
each with:
- :db/ident (the keyword name)
- :db/valueType (what values it accepts)
- :db/cardinality (:one or :many)
- :db/resolutionMode (:lww, :lattice, or :multi)
- :db/layerIndex (which schema layer)

## Determinism (INV-STORE-008, INV-LAYOUT-007)
The genesis transaction is embedded as compile-time constant bytes.
Store::genesis() deserializes it and transacts it.
Any two calls to genesis() produce IDENTICAL stores (byte-for-byte).

## The 17 Attributes
| Attribute | ValueType | Cardinality | Layer |
|-----------|-----------|-------------|-------|
| :db/ident | Keyword | One | Meta |
| :db/valueType | Keyword | One | Meta |
| :db/cardinality | Keyword | One | Meta |
| :db/resolutionMode | Keyword | One | Meta |
| :db/doc | String | One | Meta |
| :db/unique | Boolean | One | Meta |
| :db/latticeOrder | Ref | Many | Meta |
| :db/layerIndex | Long | One | Meta |
| :db/peerAttribute | Ref | One | Meta |
| :db/inverseName | Keyword | One | Meta |
| :spec/type | Keyword | One | Core |
| :spec/id | String | One | Core |
| :spec/statement | String | One | Core |
| :spec/namespace | Keyword | One | Core |
| :spec/traces-to | String | Many | Core |
| :tx/agent | Ref | One | Agent |
| :tx/provenance | Keyword | One | Agent |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Genesis transaction defines exactly 17 attributes
- [ ] Each attribute has all required metadata
- [ ] genesis() == genesis() (determinism)
- [ ] Schema::from_datoms(genesis_store) produces valid Schema
- [ ] Meta-schema self-description: schema attributes describe themselves

## Traces To
spec/02-schema.md §2.4 (genesis), ADR-SCHEMA-002, INV-SCHEMA-002, INV-SCHEMA-005

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: GENESIS: Bootstrap transaction containing 17 axiomatic attributes as datoms. Each attribute self-describes: e.g., the :db/valueType attribute has :db/valueType=Keyword. Chicken-and-egg resolved by genesis being a compile-time constant (known byte sequence). Genesis file: .braid/genesis.ednl (content-addressed, deterministic). All stores start from genesis. Schema validation only applies AFTER genesis (genesis is the axiom). File: braid-kernel/src/schema.rs.

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: 17 AXIOMATIC ATTRIBUTES DECOMPOSITION: 9 :db/* meta-schema (ident, valueType, cardinality, doc, unique, isComponent, resolutionMode, latticeOrder, lwwClock) + 5 :lattice/* definition (ident, elements, comparator, bottom, top) + 3 :tx/* metadata (time, agent, provenance). All compile-time constants. Genesis hash = BLAKE3(canonical_edn(all_17_attr_datoms)).

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn
comments.text: CRITICAL CORRECTION (audit pass 3): Genesis attributes are WRONG in the bead body. The 5 :spec/* attributes must be replaced with 5 :lattice/* attributes per guide/02-schema.md: :lattice/ident (String :one), :lattice/elements (String :many), :lattice/comparator (String :one), :lattice/bottom (String :one), :lattice/top (String :one). The 9 :db/* attrs must include :db/isComponent (Boolean :one) and :db/lwwClock (Keyword :one), and must NOT include :db/peerAttribute, :db/inverseName, :db/layerIndex which are phantom attributes not in the guide. Correct decomposition: 9 :db/* (ident, valueType, cardinality, doc, unique, isComponent, resolutionMode, latticeOrder, lwwClock) + 5 :lattice/* + 3 :tx/* (time, agent, provenance) = 17 total. Genesis also creates 1 system agent entity (3 datoms: :agent/ident, :agent/program, :agent/model) and 1 genesis transaction metadata entity. Total genesis datom count must be explicitly specified and deterministically verifiable. Genesis hash = BLAKE3(canonical_edn(all_genesis_datoms)).

title: Implement schema validation, monotonicity, and lattice definitions
description:
# Schema Validation, Monotonicity, and Lattice Definitions

## Schema Validation (INV-SCHEMA-004)
Every datom transacted into the store is validated against the schema:
1. Attribute must exist in schema
2. Value type must match schema's declared valueType
3. Cardinality :one → at most one unretracted value per (entity, attr)
If validation fails, transaction is rejected (TxValidationError).

## Schema Monotonicity (INV-SCHEMA-003)
Schema attributes only grow — you can ADD new attributes but never remove or modify
existing ones. This is enforced by the append-only store: the only way to "change"
a schema attribute is to assert a new datom, which doesn't remove the old one.

## Six-Layer Architecture (INV-SCHEMA-006)
Layer dependencies are acyclic:
- Layer N can reference attributes from layers 0..N only
- Meta (0) has no dependencies
- Agent (1) depends on Meta
- Core (2) depends on Meta, Agent
- Discovery (3) depends on Meta, Agent, Core
- etc.
Violations are SchemaError::LayerDependencyViolation.

## Lattice Definitions (INV-SCHEMA-007)
For ResolutionMode::Lattice, the lattice definition must be complete:
- Partial order is defined (via :db/latticeOrder refs)
- Join (least upper bound) is computable for any pair
- Bottom element exists
If incomplete, fallback to MultiValue resolution.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Transaction validation rejects unknown attributes
- [ ] Transaction validation rejects type mismatches
- [ ] Schema only grows (no attribute deletion)
- [ ] Layer dependency violations detected
- [ ] Lattice completeness verified for Lattice-mode attributes

## Traces To
spec/02-schema.md §2.5-2.8, guide/02-schema.md

comments.author: Willem van Daalenn

title: Implement SCHEMA proptest and Kani verification suites
description:
# SCHEMA Verification Suite

## Proptest Properties
1. schema_from_genesis: Schema::from_datoms(genesis_store) has all 17 attributes
2. schema_monotonicity: after adding attributes, count only increases
3. schema_validation: invalid value types rejected
4. genesis_determinism: genesis Schema is always identical
5. meta_self_description: all schema attributes describe themselves
6. layer_acyclicity: layer references respect ordering
7. lattice_completeness: lattice-mode attrs have complete definitions

## Kani Harnesses
- inv_schema_001: schema reconstructible from datoms
- inv_schema_003: schema attribute count monotonically increasing

# QUERY Namespace: Datalog Engine + Graph Algorithms

## Scope
10 invariants at Stage 0: INV-QUERY-001, 002, 005, 006, 007, 012, 013, 014, 017, 021.
11 deferred: INV-QUERY-003, 004, 008-011, 015, 016, 018-020 (Stages 1-3).

## Two Subsystems
### 1. Datalog Engine (INV-QUERY-001, 002, 005, 006, 007)
- Parser: pest or nom (ADR-QUERY-001)
- Stratum classifier: 6 strata, evaluate S0+S1 only
- Semi-naive fixpoint evaluator with delta tracking
- CALM compliance for S0/S1 queries

### 2. Graph Algorithms (INV-QUERY-012, 013, 014, 017, 021)
DECOMPOSED into 5 independent modules (see brai-3vcb):
- topo_sort.rs: Kahn's algorithm (INV-QUERY-012)
- scc.rs: Tarjan's SCC (INV-QUERY-013)
- pagerank.rs: Power iteration (INV-QUERY-014)
- critical_path.rs: Longest path DP (INV-QUERY-017)
- density.rs: Graph metrics (INV-QUERY-021)

Plus: EntityGraph adapter for Store → GraphView conversion.

## Build Order
1. Datalog parser
2. Stratum classifier (6 strata)
3. Semi-naive evaluator
4. Graph decomposition plan (brai-3vcb)
5. Entity graph adapter (brai-3s1s)
6. Graph algorithms (brai-2mh7.1.4.4)
7. Proptest + Kani suite
